articles.title: MacBook Air M5 review: a small update for the ‘just right’ Mac
articles.description: Not much has changed in the new MacBook Air for 2026: It now has the M5 chip, Wi-Fi 7, and faster storage starting at 512GB instead of 256GB. It's just as outstanding a computer as last year's model, even if it's a shame it's $100 more expensive. The bigger change has happened to the Air. […]
articles.content: Neo to the left of me. Pros are to the right. | Photo: Antonio G. Di Benedetto / The Verge Not much has changed in the new MacBook Air for 2026: It now has the M5 chip, Wi-Fi 7, and faster storage starting at 512GB instead of 256GB. It's just as outstanding a computer as last year's model, even if it's a shame it's $100 more expensive. The bigger change has happened to the Air. The MacBook Neo now exists, and it's an awesome little computer for $500 less than the base 13-inch Air. The Neo's exis
articles.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto

articles.title: ByteDance has reportedly suspended the global rollout of its new AI video generator
articles.description: A month after Seedance 2.0's launch in China sparked cease-and-desist letters from Disney and Paramount Skydance over its use of copyrighted materials, its developer ByteDance has reportedly hit pause on the release of the AI video tool in other regions. According to The Information , which spoke to
articles.content: A month after Seedance 2.0's launch in China sparked cease-and-desist letters from Disney and Paramount Skydance over its use of copyrighted materials, its developer ByteDance has reportedly hit pause on the release of the AI video tool in other regions. According to The Information , which spoke to two anonymous sources with knowledge of the matter, ByteDance has suspended Seedance 2.0's global rollout. Engadget has reached out to ByteDance for comment and will update this story if we hear back
articles.author: Cheyenne MacDonald

articles.title: Open source for awkward robots
articles.description: Ryan is joined by Jan Liphardt, CEO and co-founder of OpenMind, to chat about the rapidly evolving world of humanoid robotics and what it means for humans, why OpenMind is building an open source operating system for robots that processes logic in natural language, and how putting Asimov’s Laws on t
articles.content: Ryan is joined by Jan Liphardt, CEO and co-founder of OpenMind, to chat about the rapidly evolving world of humanoid robotics and what it means for humans, why OpenMind is building an open source operating system for robots that processes logic in natural language, and how putting Asimov’s Laws on the blockchain might be the key to robotics guardrails.
articles.author: Phoebe Sajor

articles.title: Git Archaeology #11 — Entropy: The Universe Always Tends Toward Disorder
articles.description: Entropy always increases. Engineers fight it. Previously In Chapter 10 , I wrote about Dark Matter — work that doesn't appear in commits, invisible gravity. This time, I write about another law of the universe. The Second Law of Thermodynamics. The increase of entropy. Entropy Physics has a concept
articles.content: Entropy always increases. Engineers fight it. Previously In Chapter 10 , I wrote about Dark Matter — work that doesn't appear in commits, invisible gravity. This time, I write about another law of the universe. The Second Law of Thermodynamics. The increase of entropy. Entropy Physics has a concept called entropy. Put simply — order, left alone, always breaks down. Drop a single drop of ink into a glass of water. The ink diffuses and mixes uniformly. But the reverse never happens. Ink never spon
articles.author: machu

I don't typically gravitate toward locked room mysteries, but the description of this book ticked all the right boxes to win me over: "a death mo
articles.content: Need something new for your reading list? This week, we recommend A.D. Sui's The Iron Garden Sutra, a meditative horror sci-fi/fantasy and murder mystery. I don't typically gravitate toward locked room mysteries, but the description of this book ticked all the right boxes to win me over: "a death monk and a team of researchers trapped onboard a spaceship of the dead encounter something beyond human understanding." It has all the makings of a compelling murder mystery, which is fine on its own, b
articles.author: Cheyenne MacDonald

articles.title: It's over, Repo. I have the high ground!
articles.description: Your boyfriend has messed up. Royally. He knows it. You know it. The neighbor knows it. Everyone and their mother knows it! The words form in your head - I. Told. You. So. And then, this other voice, "Don't be a dick, Anju" seemingly comes out of nowhere! And then, dang! You swallow the sentence. Co
articles.content: Your boyfriend has messed up. Royally. He knows it. You know it. The neighbor knows it. Everyone and their mother knows it! The words form in your head - I. Told. You. So. And then, this other voice, "Don't be a dick, Anju" seemingly comes out of nowhere! And then, dang! You swallow the sentence. Congratulations. You’re basically Mother Teresa. Just... hotter. Next scenario: The man cuts you off in traffic. Your hand is already on the horn. A very specific two-word insult involving his mother… i
articles.author: Anju Karanji

According to a report from The Wall Street Journal , the Trump administration is set to receive a total of $10 billion in the deal that allowed TikTok to remain in the US. The new investors who acqu
articles.content: There may have been some extra incentive for the Trump administration to get the TikTok US deal done. According to a report from The Wall Street Journal , the Trump administration is set to receive a total of $10 billion in the deal that allowed TikTok to remain in the US. The new investors who acquired stakes in the US entity of TikTok already paid a $2.5 billion fee to the administration when the deal closed in January, but WSJ 's latest report noted that the group of investors would continue

articles.title: Meta is reportedly laying off up to 20 percent of its staff
articles.description: According to Reuters, Meta is looking to offset spending on AI and data centers with a massive round of layoffs. Sources familiar with the matter say the company could lay off as much as 20 percent of its staff, eliminating roughly 15,800 positions. That would be the largest series of layoffs at the
articles.content: According to Reuters , Meta is looking to offset spending on AI and data centers with a massive round of layoffs. Sources familiar with the matter say the company could lay off as much as 20 percent of its staff, eliminating roughly 15,800 positions. That would be the largest series of layoffs at the company since it terminated 22,000 workers over just a few months between November 2022 and early 2023 . Word of the potential downsizing comes after Meta signaled that it was all but giving up on V
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien

articles.title: Prediction markets want the Oscars to be your gateway drug to betting on everything
articles.description: Though it was weird to see the Golden Globes partner with Polymarket for its most recent awards show, the collaboration wasn't shocking given the way that people have begun using prediction markets to gamble on basically anything. Prediction markets are being used to bet on everything from when wars
articles.content: Though it was weird to see the Golden Globes partner with Polymarket for its most recent awards show , the collaboration wasn't shocking given the way that people have begun using prediction markets to gamble on basically anything. Prediction markets are being used to bet on everything from when wars might break out to who will win the Nobel Peace Prize , so why wouldn't people also be interested in placing wagers on more entertaining and less consequential things, like celebrities' chances of w
articles.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore

articles.title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
articles.description: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called Absolics is planning to start commercial production of special glass panels designed to make next-generation
articles.content: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called Absolics is planning to start commercial production of special glass panels designed to make next-generation computing hardware more powerful and energy efficient. Other companies, including Intel, are also pushing forward in this area. If all goes well, such glass technology could reduce the energy demands
articles.author: Jeremy Hsu

articles.title: Spotify’s new Taste Profile feature lets users fine-tune their algorithm’s recommendations
articles.description: You're responsible for your own Spotify algorithm now. On stage at SXSW, Spotify's co-CEO, Gustav Söderström, announced the Taste Profile feature, which allows users to personally customize exactly what they want to listen to, whether it's music, audiobooks or podcasts. This AI-powered feature is st
articles.content: You're responsible for your own Spotify algorithm now. On stage at SXSW, Spotify's co-CEO, Gustav Söderström, announced the Taste Profile feature, which allows users to personally customize exactly what they want to listen to, whether it's music, audiobooks or podcasts. This AI-powered feature is still in beta, and it will be available to Premium users in New Zealand in the coming weeks. From its short video demo, Spotify's Taste Profile feature will show you a summary of your listening habits a

articles.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
articles.description: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s manufacturing leaders face a different challenge: how to grow amid labor constraints, rising complexity, and i
articles.content: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s manufacturing leaders face a different challenge: how to grow amid labor constraints, rising complexity, and increasing pressure to innovate faster without sacrificing safety, quality, or trust. The next phase of transformation will not be defined by isolated AI tools or individual robots, but by intelligence
articles.author: Dayan Rodriguez

articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. articles.title: Teaching an AI to Play Dwarf Fortress: The Idea
articles.description: I'm Ryan Miller. I've been a software engineer for over twenty years, mostly building web applications, mostly on the front end. I'm also a fantasy nerd -- I play tabletop RPGs, paint miniatures, read way too many fantasy novels. I'd already been using LLMs to build AI-powered tools for my tabletop
articles.content: I'm Ryan Miller. I've been a software engineer for over twenty years, mostly building web applications, mostly on the front end. I'm also a fantasy nerd -- I play tabletop RPGs, paint miniatures, read way too many fantasy novels. I'd already been using LLMs to build AI-powered tools for my tabletop hobby, and I wanted to try something harder. Not just extracting data, but making decisions in a live, complex system. What if I built an AI that plays Dwarf Fortress? Why Dwarf Fortress? If you haven
articles.author: Ryan Miller

articles.title: Staff complain that xAI is flailing because of constant upheaval
articles.description: Staff complain that the constant upheaval is destroying morale.
articles.content: Elon Musk has ordered another round of job cuts at xAI after growing frustrated with the poor performance of its coding product, forcing out several more cofounders and parachuting in “fixers” from SpaceX and Tesla to audit the startup. The latest overhaul of the 2-year-old startup follows the success of Anthropic and OpenAI, whose AI coding tools have shaken up the software industry, multiple people familiar with the decisions said. Musk has dialled up the pressure after merging SpaceX with xAI
articles.author: Stephen Morris and Cristina Criddle, Financial Times

articles.title: NASA officials sidestepped questions on Artemis II risks—there's a reason why
articles.description: "This ought to make for some good reading," NASA's mission management team chair said.
articles.content: When talking about risk during a press conference on Thursday, the NASA officials in charge of the upcoming Artemis II Moon mission hedged their answers. Reporters' questions on the risks were certainly valid and appropriate. In an open society, it is vital to set expectations for any hazardous venture such as spaceflight—most importantly for the astronauts actually making the journey, but also for NASA's workforce, the White House, lawmakers, and members of the public paying for the endeavor. W
articles.author: Stephen Clark

articles.title: Slay the Spire 2 is a bit too familiar for its own good
articles.description: Early Access impressions: New characters shine, but it feels like we've done this before.
articles.content: Do you remember the joyful satisfaction you felt when you really started to understand Slay the Spire ? This isn’t a totally rhetorical question. If you’re reading this piece about Slay the Spire 2 —published roughly a week into what promises to be a lengthy Early Access period —I have to assume you’ve put in dozens, if not hundreds (or thousands?) of hours with the original Slay the Spire . At this point, the game probably feels less like a game and more like a comfortable old pair of sneakers.
articles.author: Kyle Orland

Speaking of Google, you can also currently save $60 on the Pixel Watch 4 ; there are also savings to be had on microSD Express cards , ice cream makers , and a host of other great tech th

articles.title: Woman sneezes out maggots after fly larvae get trapped in her deviated septum
articles.description: She made a full recovery, despite the maggots.
articles.content: A 58-year-old woman in Greece appears to hold the record for growing a parasitic sheep bot fly in her nose the longest, almost creating a snot rocket that could literally fly. Usually, when the sheep bot fly accidentally nosedives into a human's schnoz, the first-stage larvae they deliver don’t actually develop. In contrast, in its normal target—a sheep's nose— the larvae would move up into the sinuses, feed, grow, and molt into second- and third-stage larvae. From there, the flies ( Oestrus ovi
articles.author: Beth Mole
